data.title: Western Digital, Seagate maintain clear path to higher earnings; BofA ups price objectives
data.description:
Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX) both maintain clear trajectories to higher earnings, prompting Bank of America to increase its price objectives on both stocks.

"Hard disk drive supply remains tight as manufacturers are not adding unit capacity, and we see this as a structural change," said BofA analysts, led by Wamsi Mohan, in a Monday investor note. "As such, we continue to see demand exceeding supply and see continued scope for OEMs to raise prices."

BofA increased its price objective on Seagate to $700 from $605 and Western Digital to $495 from $415. In a bull case scenario, BofA finds that both companies could see significantly higher earnings per share totals by 2028.

"We estimate that the current stock price of around $587 factors in a C28 EPS of $29 (derived using nearline $/TB CAGR of 4% and C28 OM of 47%) and assuming a 20x multiple (which we see as appropriate for the growth and margin profile of STX)," the analysts said of Seagate. "But, in a bull case, we see the potential for EPS of $45 ($/TB reaching $20 in C28 with a 3-year $/TB CAGR of 16% and OM of 55%)."

For Western Digital, BofA said, "We estimate that the current stock price of around $400 factors in a C28 EPS of about $20 (derived using nearline $/TB CAGR of 0% (no growth) and C28 OM of 45%) and assuming a 20x multiple (which we see as appropriate for the growth and margin profile of WDC). But, in a bull case, we see the potential for EPS of $33 ($/TB reaching $20 in C28 with a 3-year CAGR of 12% and OM of 55%)."

Visibility is also improving for both companies as they lock in long-term contracts with customers.

"The market remains an oligopoly (with three OEMs providing hard disk drives), and we see a low threat of new entrants coming into the market," Wamsi added.

The share prices of Seagate and Western Digital have both more than doubled year-to-date.

data.title: How to play the K-shaped economy in the restaurant sector
data.description:
JPMorgan believes the K-shaped economy remains firmly in place, especially in the restaurant sector.

Analyst John Ivankoe noted that the top 40% of consumers, who generate approximately 65% of all restaurant sales, are seeing increased spending power, supported by rising home values, robust stock market participation, and outsized benefits from recent tax policy changes and refunds. Meanwhile, Ivankoe highlighted that the bottom 60% of consumers are experiencing stagnant wage growth, with many unable to participate in recent wealth creation. "About 35% of households have not benefited from asset price appreciation. Legislative changes — including the expiration of ACA tax credits and expanded SNAP work requirements — are expected to further pressure this cohort," he warned.

Adding it all up, Ivankoe and his team said the combined effect of the policies is net negative for the bottom 70% of income earners, while only the top 30% can offset headwinds through tax cuts and market asset gains.

The recommendation to investors is to add to positions in its favorite names, such as Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Dutch Bros (BROS), and Brinker International (EAT). While the level of Treasury yields impacts restaurant sector valuations, the firm still sees value in shares of Darden Restaurants (DRI), McDonald's (MCD), Restaurant Brands International (QSR), and Yum! Brands (YUM). While noting valuation is stretched, JPMorgan is sticking with bullish views on CAVA Group (CAVA) and Starbucks (SBUX). The firm is less positive on Bloomin' Brands (BLMN), Cheesecake Factory (CAKE), Krispy Kreme (DNUT), and Wendy's (WEN).

data.title: Goldman Sachs cuts IPO forecast; biggest IPOs since 2025 ranked by market cap
data.description:
Goldman Sachs cut its 2026 IPO forecast, now expecting about 100 deals raising $160 billion, down from a prior estimate of 120 offerings, citing geopolitical uncertainty and recent equity market volatility. The bank said the macro backdrop remains supportive but flagged increased selectivity and downside risks to issuance.

Here is the list of the biggest IPOs since 2025, ranked by market cap:

  • Venture Global (VG) – $60B | YTD +74.49% | Since IPO -48%
  • Medline (MDLN) – $38B | YTD +6.57% | Since IPO +59%
  • Figma (FIG) – $16B | YTD -53.25% | Since IPO -48%
  • CoreWeave (CRWV) – $14B | YTD +53.81% | Since IPO +194%
  • Madison Air Solutions (MAIR) – $13B | YTD N/A | Since IPO +27%
  • SailPoint (SAIL) – $13B | YTD -40.93% | Since IPO -50%
  • Fermi (FRMI) – $12B | YTD -32.50% | Since IPO -72%
  • Arxis (ARXS)—$11B | YTD N/A | Since IPO +33%
  • Chime Financial (CHYM) – $10B | YTD -10.45% | Since IPO -16%
  • StubHub (STUB) – $9B | YTD -47.15% | Since IPO -70%
  • Forgent Power Solutions (FPS) – $8B | YTD N/A | Since IPO +28%
  • Smithfield Foods (SFD) – $7B | YTD +31.44% | Since IPO +46%
  • Netskope (NTSK) – $7B | YTD -45.10% | Since IPO -48%
  • BETA Technologies (BETA) – $7B | YTD -45.80% | Since IPO -55%
  • Circle Internet Group (CRCL) – $7B | YTD +25.67% | Since IPO +223%
  • Firefly Aerospace (FLY) – $6B | YTD +57.04% | Since IPO -14%
  • Navan (NAVN) – $6B | YTD -8.67% | Since IPO -38%
  • NIQ Global Intelligence (NIQ) – $6B | YTD -29.96% | Since IPO -47%
  • EquipmentShare.com (EQPT) – $6B | YTD N/A | Since IPO 0%
  • Caris Life Sciences (CAI) – $6B | YTD -25.13% | Since IPO -6%

data.title: Critical Metals to buy European Lithium in $835M deal, taking full Tanbreez ownership
data.description:
Critical Metals (CRML) up 7.2% in early trading Monday after saying it agreed to acquire all shares of Australian-listed miner ​European Lithium (EULIF) for ~$835M, giving ⁠the U.S. company full ​ownership of Greenland's Tanbreez ​rare earth project.

Critical Metals (CRML) already owned a ​92.5% interest in the ​asset, with European Lithium (EULIF) holding the remaining 7.5% in the project.

Under the deal terms, ​European ​Lithium (EULIF) ⁠shareholders would receive 0.035 shares of ​Critical Metals (CRML) for each ​share held.

Critical Metals (CRML) says the Tanbreez deposit, which contains rare earths such as terbium and dysprosium used in consumer electronics and defense technology, is one of the world's largest known rare earth assets.

The company noted that European Lithium (EULIF) has a ~A$306M (US$219M) cash balance as of March 31; Critical Metals (CRML), whose standalone cash balance totals US$124M, said it will have a robust balance sheet to accelerate the development of Tanbreez.

data.title: Wall Street mixed ahead of Big Tech earnings; Iran developments in focus
data.description:
The stock market opened mixed on Monday as investors looked forward to Big Tech earnings slated to come in this week and continued to monitor developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was flat, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.3%.

Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta (META) are scheduled to report results on Wednesday, followed by Apple (AAPL) the next day.

Meanwhile, Iran gave the U.S. a new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, with nuclear negotiations postponed for a later stage, according to Axios.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose to 3.80%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.32% and the 30-year yield (US30Y) advanced to 4.93%, reflecting a modest uptick as investors assessed the macro backdrop and rate outlook.

data.title: Rubrik initiated with Buy, Commvault at Hold at Jefferies
data.description:
Jefferies started coverage of cybersecurity companies Rubrik (RBRK) with a Buy and Commvault Systems (CVLT) with a Hold rating.

The firm also has a Buy rating on AvePoint (AVPT) with a $16 price target.

Rubrik (RBRK)

Jefferies set a $65 price target on Rubrik's stock.

"As a cyber resilience leader, RBRK is well positioned for LT sustainable growth, driven by the secular tailwinds of ransomware, SaaS/cloud workloads, & data growth. We see upside to cons ARR [Annual Recurring Revenue] estimates in FY27 & believe RBRK can sustain Sub ARR growth of 20%+ the next few years, driven by cross-sell of SaaS/cloud data protection, cyber resilience capabilities, & data/AI governance," said analysts led by Joseph Gallo.

The analysts added that while Rubrik continues to lag cyber peers at a similar revenue scale inoperating profitability, they see a path to significant improvement. The analysts view Rubrik's stock as attractive, with faster consensus revenue growth and margin expansion.

Commvault (CVLT)

The firm has a $105 price target on Commvault's stock.

The analysts said Commvault has evolved from a traditional backup vendor to a cyber resilience platform, which is driving growth and market share gains. The company has introduced new cybersecurity solutions, creating opportunities for cross-selling, but it remains early, the analysts added.

"CVLT’s transition to a subscription and SaaS model creates further expansion opportunities and improves visibility into its business (SaaS ARR has increased from 9% in FY22 to 33.5% as of F3Q26). At 13.3x EV/CY27E FCF, the valuation is defensive (especially given takeout reporting by Reuters, which CVLT has not commented on), and we see a path to LT sustainable growth; however, we see little upside to F4Q (CVLT reaffirmed guidance) and some risk to consensus FY27 Sub ARR growth," said Gallo and his team.

AvePoint (AVPT)

Gallo and his team noted that AvePoint has an established position in the enterprise segment and has been growing its position in the small and medium-sized business, or SMB, segment for the data backup and recovery market.

"It is focusing on increasing its SMB penetration, while maintaining and expanding its enterprise base. AvePoint should be a beneficiary of consolidating data management spend via its platform as enterprise and SMB customers struggle to manage their data sprawl in the wake of AI implementation. We believe shares are attractively valued for 26% yoy cc ARR growth at 3.5x CY26 EV/revenue estimates vs. its peer group average trading at 11.2x on revenue growth of 20.3%," the analysts noted.

Shares of Rubrik rose about 1%, while AvePoint was also in the green. Commvault dipped about 1% on Monday.

data.title: Customers Bancorp partners with OpenAI for AI deployment across commercial banking unit
data.description:
Customers Bancorp (CUBI) announced a multi-year strategic collaboration between its unit, Customers Bank, and OpenAI (OPENAI) for the deployment of AI across commercial banking operations.

CUBI shares were 1.22% higher at $75.29 during Monday pre-market trading.

The partnership is expected to position Customers Bank as one of the first AI-enabled regional banks in the country.

The current announcement expands the relationship between the two companies, which began in 2023 when Customers Bank chose to deploy ChatGPT Enterprise.

The partnership involves direct onsite engagement with OpenAI's technical teams and resources.

Customers Bank will now seek to reimagine its lending, deposit, and payment lifecycles, with strict data governance and regulatory transparency at its core.

data.title: Super Micro adds largest Silicon Valley campus to advance delivery of next-generation AI data centers
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (SMCI) announced on Monday its largest US location with the advanced DCBBS campus and a significant expansion of its Silicon Valley operations with a new state-of-the-art business complex near its headquarters in San Jose, California.  
  • The company stated that the latest addition, spanning approximately 32.8 acres and more than 714,000 square feet, marked its fourth Bay Area site and brought its regional footprint to nearly 4 million square feet.
  • The facilities will support a full range of domestic operations, including advanced system design, manufacturing, testing, and service, as well as global distribution of the company's DCBBS for AI infrastructure, it added.
  • The company is expected to create hundreds of new high-quality jobs across engineering, manufacturing, and business functions, reinforcing Supermicro's long-term commitment to local workforce development and U.S.-based production.
  • As global demand for AI infrastructure accelerates, the company is scaling its domestic capabilities to meet the needs of enterprises and cloud providers deploying increasingly complex, compute-intensive workloads.
  • Super Micro Computer said the new facilities enhance its ability to support hyperscale, cloud, and enterprise customers building AI factories, while reinforcing its role as a U.S.-based manufacturing leader in high-performance computing infrastructure.
  • SMCI -0.44% premarket to $28.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Equities shrug off oil spike, but rates and concentration risks loom, Timmer says - CNBC intervoew
data.description:
Stock markets have largely shrugged off the recent oil price spike, but concentration risk and rising interest rates could still derail the rally, according to Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity Investments.

While Brent crude remains near $100 per barrel, equities have recovered quickly, similar to the pattern seen during the 1990 Gulf War, when oil prices doubled and then reversed.

The strategist said in an interview with CNBC that the Federal Reserve has limited options when dealing with supply-driven inflation. “The Fed acknowledges that they’re powerless over supply shocks. So raising rates because oil prices are up, I don’t think that really serves much of a purpose,” he explained.

However, he noted that the three rate cuts expected earlier this year are no longer likely, nor are they necessary given current economic conditions.

Oil markets themselves are pricing in a resolution to current supply concerns. The front-month contract is trading about $20 higher than contracts a year out, suggesting traders expect prices to normalize.

“That is something that will need to happen before the markets can really completely declare victory,” Timmer said.

Looking ahead, Timmer identified two primary concerns for equity markets. First is concentration risk, as the recent 17% decline in the Magnificent Seven stocks from their October highs demonstrated how heavily these names can pull down broader indices.

Second is interest rate risk—if the 10-year yield rises to 4.5% or 5%, stock valuations would face pressure as risk-free assets become more competitive.

For now, earnings growth of over 20% is supporting valuations and keeping the market buoyant. “If the earnings do not come through, that’s going to be a problem because then the market will just become more expensive because the E is not doing the heavy lifting,” he warned.

However, current data shows earnings estimates still accelerating, providing a cushion against potential headwinds.

The analyst also addressed the incoming Fed leadership, noting that Kevin Warsh’s known opposition to quantitative easing and plans to reduce the Fed’s balance sheet could face market tests.

While new Fed chairs typically face a market correction averaging 15%, bond markets rather than stocks may be the arena where any policy disagreements play out.
